missed_changes: Newly prominent, repeated excitement about on-chain / web3 gaming as a favorite sector (composability, mutating state, games on L2s) — not anticipated
Bullish stance on NFT/creator-economy rebound: patronage models, open editions, lower take rates, primary-sale creator relationships

Elevated application-layer privacy as a near-term deliverable (Nocturne, Cicada private voting, zkDocs, private airdrops, 'privacy is coming to crypto') beyond the prediction's 'eventually privacy' framing
New emphasis on decentralized/open social networks and 'network architecture resists centralization' (pro-Farcaster, critiques of Nostr/Fediverse degrading to centralization)
New stance advocating Bitcoin programmability via SNARKs/ZK (VERIFY_SNARK opcode, 'bring zero knowledge home to bitcoin') plus concern about Bitcoin's long-term security model
New interest in on-chain IP / composable rights graphs (Story Protocol)
New theme: AI/AGI and using crypto + ZK for content provenance against deepfakes
notes: Window covers Apr–Sep 2023. The three excited sectors (ZK, account abstraction/smart accounts, L2 scaling) are heavily and repeatedly confirmed — ZK via Lasso/Jolt, zkDocs, trusted setups and the State of Crypto ZK slide; smart accounts via the July smart-account/AA threads; L2 via the State of Crypto report, podcast, Magi/OP Stack, EIP-4844/proto-danksharding enthusiasm. US regulatory concern is confirmed through the State of Crypto report he co-authored ('U.S. is losing its lead,' developer share fell 26%, lack of clarity hindering growth) — same stance as the prediction though framed as lack-of-clarity rather than enforcement specifically. ETH marked confirmed: he repeatedly and strongly endorses Ethereum and explicitly welcomes 'the recent upgrade' (Shapella) and the roadmap, though as a builder he frames bullishness through technology/scaling rather than the asset's monetary thesis (no price/asset commentary, per his disclaimers). The three March-specific concerns about banking fragility — centralized stablecoin banking dependency, US fiat ramps (Silvergate/Signature), and CeFi custody/counterparty risk — go entirely unaddressed in the window; the Sept security post and DEX>CEX comparison touch self-custody but not as a CEX counterparty-risk concern, so all three are absent (none contradicted). Risk regime confirmed: consistent 'zoom out from price volatility, fundamentals are healthy, keep building' messaging matches a constructive-on-tech, deploy-through-cycle, non-aggressive posture. tokens_concerned was empty so nothing to evaluate there.
